Hey, Jordan Belford here again and welcome back to the straight line persuasion fast track. A system designed to teach any person how to truly become a world class, close, in which in my book means being able to close anyone who's closeable. So now that you have an introduction to the system, I want to dig a little bit deeper here and go into a subject here that I'm sure it's a word that you've heard before, but my book is one of the most misunderstood or misused words in the English language and that is this idea of getting into rapport with people. And that's what this module is going to focus on, how to get into rapport on both a conscious and an unconscious level. Now in the last module, if you recall, we went through these three basic tenants of the straight line system. And this is really what I said to my brokers right from the gate when I said to them that, you know, listen, every sales the same, every sale, every negotiation, every situation of influence, every single one is exactly the same. And I gave me three basic tenants of the system and I drew that straight line on the board. Let's put up a graphic here where I put the straight line on the board and I said, there's an open and there's a close and your job essentially is to move your client down the straight line from the open to the close. And as you're doing that, you're building up this massive certainty, right, both emotional and logical certainty. Then you have these boundaries above and below the line. And that represents being in control of the sale. So you have the boundary above and if you go past that you're in Pluto, then you have the boundary below. And if you go past that one, you're in your anus, right? Not a good place to be. And that essentially is the first basic tenant of straight line persuasion. And that is that you must control the linguistic encounter. You have to control the sale. If the customer or the prospect is controlling the sale, good luck trying to close them. So again, and we have to immediately get in control of that sale. That's the first basic tenant. The second tenant, we then once we're in control, we then get into this immediate, this conscious and unconscious rapport. Okay. And at the highest level, remember what you're trying to establish in the prospect's mind essentially is that you are a person worth listening to. And really higher even than that, you want to chunk it up, is that because you're a person that can help them achieve their goals. You can help them get what they want. And without being in rapport with somebody, you know, it's very, very difficult to influence anybody to do anything. So that's your second basic tenant of the straight line system. So number one, you want to control that sale. Number two, you got to get it to instant rapport on both the conscious and unconscious level. And that leads us to the third tenant of straight line persuasion on the front end. And that is once you're in rapport, you use that rapport to now gather intelligence. And notice when I talk about gathering intelligence, when straight line intelligence gather mode, it's not just about identifying needs. It's about their pain points. It's about their beliefs. It's about understanding their values, their hierarchy of values. As a straight line prospector, when you're qualifying, you're gathering that intelligence, it is absolutely crucial that you dig deeper than the average sales person ever would. Because once you truly understand what a person's core needs are, what their pain points are, where their values lie, what their highest value is, it then allows you essentially to design such as to retrofit your solution to meet their needs. To solve their pain. And also, it allows you tend to take them out of the loop. And if they don't qualify, or you can't help them, the money, whatever it might be, then you want to get them out of the sales funnel. See what happens very often with people who have not been through straight line training, they will let anybody go down the sales funnel. They don't care if the person really needs it, qualifies or not. They get to pitch that person no matter what. That's a big waste of time. And it's also not ethical. So again, let's go again one more time. The three tenants, number one, you want to control that sale. And that comes down to being an expert and being sharp on the ball of how you're perceived, right? And you've learned that in the last module. Number two, we got to get into that instant rapport, which we're going to go through in this module. And that means on both a conscious and an unconscious level, conscious being there, they're saying to us, wow, you know, I like this person. Person's making sense. I feel like there's some likability here. They feel like they have that in my best interest at heart, something that they're thinking consciously. And then this unconscious rapport, where they just, they don't know, they just get this feeling in their gut that they're some, they're in someone you're like them. They just stay connect with you. So you want to do it consciously and unconsciously. And then the third aspect is gathering intelligence. Those three basic tenants of straight line persuasion really drive things home and it sets you up to be able to go to the second half of the straight line. We make your presentation and you solve their pain and so on and so forth. Now one of the things that makes the straight line system different from basically any system out there is that it's goal oriented. And I always refer to it as goal oriented communication, meaning this specific goal and endpoint in mind. And we focus on that endpoint and we essentially work backwards. And we say to ourselves, what are the things that must line up for us to achieve that endpoint to get to that goal every single time? And in fact, what it comes down to is actually these five levers, these core elements of influence that must line up in order to close a deal. And the metaphor that I always like to use is if you're a safe cracker in the movie The Italian Job where Staddle Sutherland is one of these old fashioned safe crackers. And what he does is he's trying to crack a safe. He puts his ear to the tumbler and he spins the dial and he has a click, right? And he's got the first number. But does the safe open? No, if he actually pulled the lever, it would still be locked. But does that mean he didn't make progress? No, of course he made a lot of progress. What does he do now? He spins the dial in the other direction and then he has a second click, crack. Does it open? No, it's still not. There's two numbers. He's got three to go. He spins it in the other direction and he spins it in the other direction. Finally, when he's got those five numbers all lined up, pulls that lever, the safe door opens. That's exactly what's going on when you take someone essentially as he's further down the straight line from the open to the close. We're actually lining up these five core elements of human influence that must be lined up before someone says yes. And at the heart of all this is something I mentioned before and it's called certainty. Meaning when you meet someone, you're trying to sell them something and you're just immediately from the beginning they're a prospect, right? They walk in with a certain level of certainty. I mean, it's not like they walk in like out of space. They walk in, they know something about your product. There's some preconceived notion they have about what you're selling, what you're recommending. Imagine a continuum of certainty where you have a one on one end which represents absolute uncertainty, then a line going out to the other end. You have a 10 on the other end and that's absolute certainty. It's the best thing since sliced bread. So one, they hate it, 10, they love it. Your goal as you're moving someone down the straight line is to get them from a one to a 10 in certainty, right? And I always say that sales at the highest level is the transference of emotion and that primary emotion you're transferring is certainty. There are different types of certainty. We'll get into this in the clips. There's emotional certainty and there's logical certainty. In order to close at the highest level, you've got to have both. But for now, I don't want to just overcomplicate it yet. What I want you to understand is this. There are these five elements and it's based on certainty and as you move someone down the straight line from the open to the close, what you're doing essentially is you're cracking their buying combination. You're lining up all five of these core elements and when you finally do and you ask for the order, guess what happens? They say, yeah, let's do it. So here's what we're going to do now. I want you to start by reading through the ebook chapter, which is going to reinforce these core elements of rapport, which will allow you to really stay in control and really stay in charge of the sale as you move it down the straight line. Then I'm going to focus in on these two other core elements of those five. The first is called the action threshold. What this is is the level of certainty that a person must be at before they say yes. In other words, some people like myself as a very low action threshold, I don't have to be all that certain. I just have to be reasonably certain and I'll say, yeah, let's do it. Then you have people like my father who is a very high action threshold and he has to be absolutely certain and damn sure of it before he'll ever have even a chance of saying yes. That's a high action threshold. Now we have people with low action thresholds out there, we have people with high action thresholds. So in this chapter, we're going to discuss the action threshold and I'll give you some very powerful strategies of how you actually lower someone's action threshold. Then the next thing we'll talk about is the pain threshold. And this refers to essentially that when people are in pain, they're feeling some discomfort in a certain aspect of their life, especially related to your product, that you can feel a need that will resolve their pain, it makes people want to take action. And actually the pain threshold, it affects the action threshold. So if someone with a very high action threshold is feeling pain, it temporary lowers their action threshold. Now, don't worry if it sounds confusing, it's actually very, very simple. And when you read this in the e-book, you're going to see just how it unfolds and how you can use these two of the five levers of influence to really get to that point where you truly can close anyone who's closeable. And then next we're going to go to a video. This is actually something that I did in Brisbane. It's a business event, a private event I did in Brisbane where you'll actually see me talking about these three core elements. The first three, because I gave you the second of the five, I gave you an action in pain, right? That's four and five. Now I'm going to go through the first three elements, the core elements of human influence. And these are the three things that must line up in every single sale. And when you see me do this live, I would do it into a camera like this as I'm talking now to the barrel of a camera, but the truth is I personally can't get to the same level of intensity and certainties I can do in front of a live audience. That's why I tend to cut back to live footage and I want to really reinforce the point and bring it home for you. So this particular event here, I was really on that day and I'm talking about these three core elements and I'm sure that when you're done with it and you get the action threshold and pain threshold, you'll see that those five elements together are literally going to change your entire perspective on how you go about influencing people. And when taking in the overall context of the straight line, the impact it will have on your clothes may be truly staggering. Now this is the first step so I want you to enjoy it, I want you to dive into it and I'll see you on the other side.
